\centering
\begin{threeparttable}
\caption{Diagnostic decompositions of the TWFE estimator (staggered articles)}
\label{tab:bacon_summary}
\small
\begin{tabular}{lc}
\toprule
\textbf{Statistic} & \textbf{Value} \\
\midrule
\multicolumn{2}{l}{\textit{Bacon decomposition ($N = 26$)}} \\
\quad Mean treated-vs-treated share & 0.349 \\
\quad Articles with TvT $> 50\%$ & 8 / 26 (31\%) \\[6pt]
\multicolumn{2}{l}{\textit{de Chaisemartin--d'Haultfoeuille weights ($N = 27$)}} \\
\quad Articles with any negative weight & 21 / 27 (78\%) \\
\quad Mean share of negative weights & 0.064 \\
\quad Median share of negative weights & 0.025 \\
\quad Maximum share of negative weights & 0.238 \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\begin{tablenotes}[flushleft]
\footnotesize
\item \textit{Notes.} Both panels are restricted to the 29 staggered-adoption articles in the comparable subsample. The Goodman-Bacon decomposition \parencite{goodman2021difference} expresses TWFE as a weighted average of $2\times 2$ comparisons. The treated-vs-treated (TvT) share collects the ``Earlier vs Later'' and ``Later vs Earlier'' components in which already-treated units serve as controls. The de Chaisemartin and d'Haultfoeuille decomposition \parencite{dechaisemartin2020twfe} expresses TWFE as a weighted sum of group-time ATTs and reports the share of negative weights as $|\Sigma\,\omega^{-}| / (|\Sigma\,\omega^{-}| + \Sigma\,\omega^{+})$. The Bacon panel loses 3 articles (panel size or no treatment variation in a balanced panel). The dCdH panel loses 2 (complex multi-file data construction). Appendix~\ref{AppendixC} gives the implementation and exclusion rules.
\end{tablenotes}
\end{threeparttable}
\end{table}
